content.full_text:
January 25, 1982

Honorable Roy G. Pucci  
Judge of the Municipal Court  
39439 Paseo Padre Parkway  
Fremont, CA 94538

Re: Disclosure of Trusts

Dear Judge Pucci:

Thank you for your telephone call requesting the Commission's advice regarding reporting of interests in trusts. As you related them over the phone, the facts are as follows:

Your wife's mother is the current beneficiary of a trust. The trustee is your wife. The principal of the trust consists of stocks. The trust is an irrevocable trust which will dissolve upon the death of your wife's mother, at which time 50 percent of the principal will devolve to your wife (or, in the event that she predeceases her mother, to your children). Your wife receives some payment as trustee but otherwise receives no income from the trust.

The Commission has adopted a regulation (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18234) to govern the reporting of interests in a trust. Given the above facts and the provisions of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18234, you should report as follows on your Form 721.

1. On Schedule D - Your community property share (1/2) of her income as trustee, provided that such income equalled at least $500 during the reporting period.

2. On Schedule C - Your wife's 1/2 interest in each of the stocks held by the trust, provided that her 1/2 interest is equal to at least $1,000. Remember that certain investments in mutual funds, etc., as defined in Government Code Section 82034, need not be disclosed.

Honorable Roy G. Pucci  
January 25, 1982  
Page Two

Should you have further questions please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Robert E. Leidigh  
Counsel  
Legal Division

REL:plh
